info.title: Bump.sh Api
info.description:
This is the official Bump.sh API documentation.  Obviously created
with Bump.sh.

The Bump.sh API is a REST API. It enables you to [create, update](#operation-post-versions)
or [preview](#operation-post-previews) your API(s) documentation,
[create stand-alone documentation diffs](#operation-post-diffs) or
[validate a documentation definition](#operation-post-validations)
(currently in OpenAPI or AsyncAPI).

Our [webhook](#webhook-documentation-change) also lets you get
notifications every time a change is introduced in your API.

tags.name: Branches
tags.description: Manage branches of a given documentation

post.description:
Create a diff between any two given API definitions.

The diff result will be available asynchronously and needs
to be retrieved with the [`GET /diffs/:id` API endpoint](#operation-get-diffs-parameter).

get.summary: Fetch detailed information from an existing diff
get.description:
Fetch the result of a previously created diff with the
[`POST /diffs` API endpoint](#operation-post-diffs).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UUID of an existing diff from which to fetch diff details

parameters.name: formats
parameters.description:
A list of formats to render in the response. Possible
values are `text`, `markdown`, `html` or `json`. If not
provided defaults to render both `text` and `markdown`
formats.

202.description: Diff is still being processed. Please try again later

get.description:
List all hubs belonging to the authenticated organization.
The API key used to make the request must be set at the organization level.

get.summary: Fetch information of an existing Hub
get.description:
Fetch information of an existing Hub including the list of
APIs it contains. The response follows the [APIs.json
specification](http://apisjson.org/).

parameters.name: hub_id_or_slug

schema.description: UUID or slug of a Hub

parameters.description: UUID or slug of a Hub which can be Automatic Deployment settings page of your hub

post.description: Deploy a new version for a given documentation, which will become the current version.

patch.summary: Promote branch as the default one

parameters.name: doc_id_or_slug

schema.description: UUID or slug of a documentation

parameters.description: UUID or slug of a documentation which can be seen in the Automatic Deployment settings page of your documentation

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Slug of a branch which can be seen in the branches settings page of your documentation

200.summary: Branch is already the default branch.

204.summary: Branch is now set to default.

patch.description: Promote branch as the default one

parameters.name: doc_id_or_slug

schema.description: UUID or slug of a documentation

parameters.description: UUID or slug of a documentation which can be seen in the Automatic Deployment settings page of your documentation

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Slug of a branch which can be seen in the branches settings page of your documentation

422.summary: The default branch can't be deleted

get.description: List the available branches of a given documentation.

parameters.name: doc_id_or_slug

schema.description: UUID or slug of a documentation

parameters.description: UUID or slug of a documentation which can be seen in the Automatic Deployment settings page of your documentation

parameters.name: doc_id_or_slug

schema.description: UUID or slug of a documentation

parameters.description: UUID or slug of a documentation which can be seen in the Automatic Deployment settings page of your documentation

post.description: Validate a definition against its schema (OpenAPI or AsyncAPI) and return errors without creating a new version. This is useful in a CI process, to validate that a changed definition file is valid and won't fail when being deployed on Bump.sh.
Create a preview for a given documentation file. The preview will have a unique temporary URL, and will be active for 30 minutes.

put.description: Update a preview with the given documentation file. The preview will stay active for 30 minutes after the last update.

parameters.name: preview_id
parameters.description: UUID of an existing preview you wish to update.

get.summary: Fetch a full documentation version including diff summary
get.description: Fetch a full documentation version including diff summary.

parameters.name: version_id

schema.description: UUID of an existing documentation version.

parameters.description: UUID of an existing version from which to fetch a documentation change

parameters.name: formats
parameters.description:
A list of formats to render in the response. Possible
values are `text`, `markdown`, `html` or `json`. If not
provided defaults to render both `text`, `markdown` and
`json` formats.

202.summary: Documentation version is still being processed. Please try again later

get.description: Responds a pong if the API is up and running.

post.summary: Deploy a new MCP server document
post.description: Deploy a new workflow document for a given MCP server.

parameters.name: mcp_server_id_or_slug
parameters.description: UUID or slug of a MCP server

definition.description: Workflow definition of the MCP server.

201.summary: MCP server workflow document successfully deployed

204.summary: MCP server workflow document already exists

post.description: Payload sent when your documentation receives a deployment with a structure change.

parameters.name: X_BUMP_SIGNATURE_256
parameters.description: The hash signature of the payload. Bump.sh uses a HMAC hex digest (SHA256) to compute the signature of the body payload with the webhook secret. More info in help.

requestBody.description: Information about last documentation structure change of your API history

previous_version_id.description: UUID of a previously deployed version

temporary.description: Whether you want the new version to be temporary or not. By default a new version will be permanent to your documentation. Temporary versions have a time to live of 7 days after which they are removed.

description.example:
# Welcome to my train company

This hub contains all APIs belonging to [my train company](https://demo.bump.sh/).
Feel free to visit the documentation or changes of our APIs lifecycle.

url.description: The public URL of the hub

modified.description: Last udpate date of this Hub

specificationVersion.description:
version of the [APIs.json
specification](http://apisjson.org/) specification in use.

apis.description: The list of APIs belonging to this Hub

description.description: Description of this API from the latest definition
description.example: This is the official Bump.sh API documentation. Obviously created with Bump.sh

tags.description: a list of descriptive strings which identify the API within a Hub

version.description: Version of this API taken from the latest definition

properties.description: Extra properties attached to this API

data.description: Content of the extra property (`data` or `url`)

url.description: Content of the extra property (`data` or `url`)

modified.description: Last udpate date of this API

location.description: Location of the external reference as it's called from `definition`, without the relative path (the part after `#/`). Can be an URL of a file system path.

content.description: Content of the external reference, as a string.

errors.description: Hash of invalid attributes with their error messages.

raw_definition: The property '#/paths//docs/{:id}/validations/post' contains additional properties ["yolo"] outside of the schema when none are allowed

message.description: Human readable error message for status 400.
message.example: You need to upgrade your subscription plan to perform this request.

pong.example: And that's how ping-pong ball is bumped

doc_public_url.description: The public URL of your documentation.

diff_details.description: Details of each change as a list of diff items

diff_markdown.description: The comparaison diff summary in markdown format
diff_markdown.example:
## Modified (1)

* `POST /user`
  * Path parameters added: `id`, `email`
  * Query parameters added: `period`, `limit`

diff_summary.example:
Updated: POST /versions
  Response modified: 201
    Body attribute added: doc_id

diff_public_url.description: The public URL of your diff

diff_breaking.description: Identifies if the diff includes breaking changes

title.description: The title of the last parsed definition

public_url.description: The public URL of your diff

breaking.description: Identifies if the diff includes breaking changes

details.description: Details of each change as a list of diff items. Present only if `json` format has been requested

previous_version_url.description: URL of previous version specification, in JSON format

current_version_url.description: URL of current version specification, in JSON format

id.description: The identifier of the diff change

name.description: The human name of diff change

type.description: The object type of the diff change

breaking.description: Identifies if the item is a breaking change

previous.description: Object attributes values, before change. Possible attributes depend on the object `type` ('endpoint', 'body', 'response'…)

current.description: Object attributes values, after change. Possible attributes depend on the object `type` ('property', 'operation', 'message'…)

breaking_details.description:
Represents breaking change reasons, used to give more
context about this question: _why is this change
breaking?_

message_key.description: This property is used to generate the human message about breaking change reason

breaking_attributes.description:
When diff status is modified, this property is an
array including every modified attribute responsible
of the breaking change.

children.description: A list of children item changes

markdown.description: The comparaison diff summary in markdown format. Present only if `markdown` format has been requested.
markdown.example:
## Modified (1)

* `POST /user`
  * Path parameters added: `id`, `email`
  * Query parameters added: `period`, `limit`

html.description: The comparaison diff summary in HTML format. Present only if `html` format has been requested.
html.example: • Added: GET /diffs/{id}

text.description: The comparaison diff summary in plain text format. Present only if `text` format has been requested.
text.example:
Updated: POST /versions
  Response modified: 201
    Body attribute added: doc_id

slug.description: Slug of the branch used in public documentation URL

is_default.description: Whether the branch is the default one or not.

documentation.description: UUID or slug of the documentation.

hub.description: UUID or slug of the hub if the documentation is part of a hub.

documentation_name.description: Name of the documentation to create. Used only if `auto_create_documentation` is set.

auto_create_documentation.description: Create the documentation if it does not exist yet. Must be used with a `hub` and a `documentation`.

specification.description: Specification of the given definition as a path: `speficiation_name/specification_version/format`.

definition.description: Serialized definition of the version. This should be an OpenAPI 2.x, 3.x or AsyncAPI 2.x file serialized as a string, in YAML or JSON.

references.description: Import external references used by `definition`. It's usually resources not accessible by Bump.sh servers, like local files or internal URLs.

branch_name.description:
Select a branch for this new version (branch will be
created if it doesn't exist).

*Defaults to the main branch*.

definition.description:
<small>==**REQUIRED**==</small> if `url` is not present.

Serialized definition. This should be an OpenAPI 2.x, 3.x
or AsyncAPI 2.x file serialized as a string, in YAML or
JSON.

references.description: Import external references used by `definition`. It's usually resources not accessible by Bump.sh servers, like local files or internal URLs.

url.description:
<small>==**REQUIRED**==</small> if `definition` is not present.

Target definition URL. It should be accessible through
HTTP by Bump.sh servers.

id.description: Unique id for the preview URL: `https://bump.sh/preview/:id`.

public_url.description: The public URL where the preview will be visible.

name.description:
The name of the branch.

> info
> Please note that the branch name is used in
> your documentation public URL

url.description:
<small>==**REQUIRED**==</small> if `definition` is not present.

Current definition URL. It should be accessible through
HTTP by Bump.sh servers.

previous_url.description:
<small>==**REQUIRED**==</small> if `definition` is not present.

Previous definition URL. It should be accessible through
HTTP by Bump.sh servers.

previous_definition.description:
<small>==**REQUIRED**==</small> if `url` is not present.

Serialized definition of the previous version. This should
be an OpenAPI 2.x, 3.x or AsyncAPI 2.x file serialized as
a string, in YAML or JSON.

previous_references.description: Import external references used by `previous_definition`. It's usually resources not accessible by Bump.sh servers, like local files or internal URLs.

definition.description:
<small>==**REQUIRED**==</small> if `url` is not present.

Serialized definition of the current version. This should
be an OpenAPI 2.x, 3.x or AsyncAPI 2.x file serialized as
a string, in YAML or JSON.

references.description: Import external references used by `definition`. It's usually resources not accessible by Bump.sh servers, like local files or internal URLs.

expires_at.description: Public change expiration date and time. After this date, this documentation change will be destroyed.

value.text:
Updated: POST /versions
  Response modified: 201
    Body attribute added: doc_id
value.markdown:
## Modified (1)

* `POST /user`
  * Path parameters added: `id`, `email`
  * Query parameters added: `period`, `limit`

value.html: • Added: GET /diffs/{id}

ExistingDocumentation.summary: Create new version on an existing documentation

CreatingStandaloneDocumentation.summary: Create a new standalone documentation with the given version

CreatingDocumentationInHub.summary: Create a new documentation inside a hub
